first_edge_evidence.recovery_entry_reason: relative yaw reached 5.1 degrees while the line remained valid

exit6_counterfactual_replay.method: Replay the recorded yaw and line samples with both zero-order hold and linear interpolation between 20 ms telemetry samples.
exit6_counterfactual_replay.conclusion: For this recorded sensor trajectory, EXIT_ERROR=6 exits recovery before the unchanged 2500 ms timeout and far below the 200 mm travel guard. A new physical run is still required because the changed firmware can produce a different vehicle trajectory.

diagnosis: Adaptive breakaway and the common launch gate worked: both wheels began moving within 20 ms and no STALL occurred. Recovery remained on a valid line and reduced yaw and wheel-count error. Center-containing mask 48/error 6 persisted from 1.10 to 2.98 seconds and yaw was already within 4 degrees at 1.94 seconds, but the old exit threshold required error <=4, so the confirmation count remained zero throughout that usable window. The line later moved to error 11 and returned to error 6 only shortly before the unchanged 2.5 second timeout fired.
corrective_action: Leave LINEKP/LINEKD unchanged. Change the recovery exit line-error threshold from 4 to 6 because mask 48 contains the center sensor, while retaining yaw <=4 degrees, heading target/output <=5 mm/s, 10 consecutive confirmations, the 2.5 second timeout, the independent 200 mm travel limit, line-loss guards and heading hard guards. Full-window replay shows that mask 48/error 6 persisted from 1.10 to 2.98 seconds and yaw returned within 4 degrees at 1.94 seconds; with the new threshold the heading target/output settle and confirmation can complete around 2.10 seconds, before the old timeout. Preserve the current yaw sample as the heading derivative origin when recovery exits.
